content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 5660 322 5901 322 6441 322 6444

May 19, 1978

Mr. Andy Kamkoff
215 Spring Drive
Boulder Creek, California 95006

Dear Mr. Kamkoff:

Thank you for your letter of May 1, 1978, requesting advice
with respect to the conflict of interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act. Specifically, you have asked whether
Director Peterson of the San Lorenzo Valley County Water District
may participate in the decision whether to provide your property
with a water connection.

Commission regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18329,
which outlines Commission advice procedure, provides that the
staff shall provide advice to officials regarding their duties
under the Political Reform Act. However, it has been the Commission's long-standing policy to refrain from advising third
persons with respect to the duties of public officials except
in the context of a complaint.

The purpose behind this policy is to provide a maximum
degree of fairness to both the public and the public official.
The Commission recognized that third persons requesting advice
regarding the activities of public officials frequently are
not in the best position to know all of the facts surrounding
the issue in question. Therefore, the Commission concluded that
it would be inappropriate to issue advice under circumstances
in which the public official had not had an opportunity to set
forth his view of the matter.

Unfortunately, it is not feasible for the Commission to
conduct a full scale investigation in every case surrounding
a request for advice. Therefore, the Commission limits its
investigative power to situations involving potential violations of the Act. In such cases, the Commission has adopted
extensive procedures to protect the rights of the person being
investigated.

Mr. Andy Kamkoff
May 19, 1978
Page Two


In light of the above discussion, I am sure you can appreciate
the problems that we would encounter if we issued advice to third
persons regarding the duties of public officials. Accordingly,
I must refrain from answering your question as to whether Director
Peterson may participate in the decision regarding the water
connection to your property. I have spoken with Mr. Amblem
from time to time regarding the conflict of interest provisions
of the Act as they apply to the activities of the water district.
Therefore, I urge that you discuss this matter with him since
he will be advising Director Peterson on whether he should parti-
cipate in this decision. Lastly, we recently received a request
for advice from Director Peterson regarding, among other things,
his participation in the decision of whether to approve a water
connection to your property. We are presently preparing a
response to Director Peterson's letter, and we will forward
a copy to you when it is complete.

I am sorry that I cannot be of further assistance, but
I hope, in light of the discussion set forth above, that you
understand the reasons why our involvement in this matter
necessarily must be limited.

Sincerely,


Ted Prim
Chief, Conflicts of
Interest Division

TF:mfa

sections.question: Whether Director Peterson of the San Lorenzo Valley County Water District may participate in the decision whether to provide your property with a water connection.
sections.conclusion: The Commission must refrain from answering the question as to whether Director Peterson may participate in the decision regarding the water connection to the property, citing its long-standing policy of not advising third persons regarding the duties of public officials except in the context of a complaint. The Commission suggests the requester discuss the matter with Mr. Amrhein, who advises Director Peterson, and notes that the Commission is preparing a response to Director Peterson's own request for advice on this matter.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks whether Director Peterson of the San Lorenzo Valley County Water District may participate in a decision regarding providing a water connection to the requester's property under the Political Reform Act's conflict of interest provisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice to the third-party requester regarding the public official's duties, citing its policy to ensure fairness and avoid situations where the public official lacks opportunity to present their view. The Commission suggests the requester speak with the official's advisor and indicates it is responding separately to the official's own advice request.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with clear Q/C content. The document explains FPPC policy rationale for declining to advise third parties. Verbatim question and conclusion sections extracted from body text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Director Peterson of the San Lorenzo Valley County Water District may participate in the decision whether to provide your property with a water connection.

CONCLUSION: The Commission must refrain from answering the question as to whether Director Peterson may participate in the decision regarding the water connection to the property, citing its long-standing policy of not advising third persons regarding the duties of public officials except in the context of a complaint. The Commission suggests the requester discuss the matter with Mr. Amrhein, who advises Director Peterson, and notes that the Commission is preparing a response to Director Peterson's own request for advice on this matter.
embedding.first_500_words: May 19, 1978 Mr. Andy Kamkoff 215 Spring Drive Boulder Creek, California 95006 Dear Mr. Kamkoff: Thank you for your letter of May 1, 1978, requesting advice with respect to the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act. Specifically, you have asked whether Director Peterson of the San Lorenzo Valley County Water District may participate in the decision whether to provide your property with a water connection. Commission regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18329, which outlines Commission advice procedure, provides that the staff shall provide advice to officials regarding their duties under the Political Reform Act. However, it has been the Commission's long-standing policy to refrain from advising third persons with respect to the duties of public officials except in the context of a complaint. The purpose behind this policy is to provide a maximum degree of fairness to both the public and the public official. The Commission recognized that third persons requesting advice regarding the activities of public officials frequently are not in the best position to know all of the facts surrounding the issue in question. Therefore, the Commission concluded that it would be inappropriate to issue advice under circumstances in which the public official had not had an opportunity to set forth his view of the matter. Unfortunately, it is not feasible for the Commission to conduct a full scale investigation in every case surrounding a request for advice. Therefore, the Commission limits its investigative power to situations involving potential violations of the Act. In such cases, the Commission has adopted extensive procedures to protect the rights of the person being investigated. Mr. Andy Kamkoff May 19, 1978 Page Two In light of the above discussion, I am sure you can appreciate the problems that we would encounter if we issued advice to third persons regarding the duties of public officials. Accordingly, I must refrain from answering your question as to whether Director Peterson may participate in the decision regarding the water connection to your property. I have spoken with Mr. Amrhein from time to time regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Act as they apply to the activities of the water district. Therefore, I suggest that you discuss this matter with him since he will be advising Director Peterson on whether he should participate in this decision. Lastly, we recently received a request for advice from Director Peterson regarding, among other things, his participation in the decision of whether to approve a water connection to your property. We are presently preparing a response to Director Peterson's letter, and we will forward a copy to you when it is complete. I am sorry that I cannot be of further assistance, but I hope, in light of the discussion set forth above, that you understand the reasons why our involvement in this matter necessarily must be limited. Sincerely, Ted Prim Chief, Conflicts of Interest Division TP:mfa
embedding.summary: Mr. Kamkoff requests FPPC advice on whether Director Peterson may participate in a water connection decision affecting his property. The FPPC declines to advise third parties about public officials' duties outside complaint contexts, but notes it is separately responding to Director Peterson's own request for advice on this matter.
